howItWorks.intro: This sea level rise impact calculator uses established formulas to provide accurate results.

rules.name: Projected Rise
rules.formula: Rise = Annual Rate × Years + Acceleration Factor
rules.explanation: Linear rise plus acceleration for longer timeframes.

howItWorks.outro: Energy costs and incentives change by region and season. Check with your local utility for current rates.

faq.question: How fast is sea level rising?
faq.answer: Currently about 3.6mm (0.14 inches) per year, accelerating over time.

faq.question: Should I worry about my coastal property?
faq.answer: Properties under 10ft elevation within 1 mile of coast face increasing risk over 30+ years.

faq.question: Is the Sea Level Rise Impact Calculator free to use?
faq.answer: Yes, completely free with no signup required. Use it as many times as you need — there are no limits or hidden fees.

faq.question: How accurate is this calculator?
faq.answer: This calculator uses standard practical calculation formulas trusted by users. Results are reliable estimates for planning purposes. For critical decisions, we recommend consulting a qualified professional to verify.

faq.question: What property elevation should I enter?
faq.answer: Enter the most accurate property elevation value you have available. If you're estimating, use a conservative figure. You can always run the calculator again with different values to see how changes affect the results.

sections.title: What Is the Sea Level Rise Impact Calculator?
sections.body: The Sea Level Rise Impact Calculator is a free online tool designed for users who need quick, accurate calculations in the practical calculation space. By entering your property elevation, distance from coast, timeframe, you get instant results including projected rise, flood risk level, safety margin. No formulas to memorize, no spreadsheets to build — just enter your numbers and get the answer in seconds. Whether you're a beginner or experienced professional, this calculator saves you time and eliminates guesswork.

sections.title: Why This Calculation Matters
sections.body: Getting projected rise right can make the difference between success and costly mistakes. In practical calculation, small errors compound quickly. Manual calculations are error-prone and time-consuming, especially under pressure. This calculator applies proven formulas used by users worldwide, giving you confidence that your numbers are correct. Use it to get accurate results with precision and avoid common pitfalls that trip up beginners.

sections.title: When Should You Use This Calculator?
sections.body: This tool is most useful when you know your property elevation and need to find the right projected rise. It's also great for quick estimates before committing to a decision, and to double-check manual calculations or professional quotes, and when comparing different scenarios side by side. Bookmark this page and come back whenever you need a fast, reliable answer — the calculator is always free and requires no signup.

howToSteps.title: Enter Your Property Elevation (ft above sea level)
howToSteps.description: Type or select your property elevation in the field provided. Use the most accurate value available for best results.

howToSteps.title: Enter Your Distance from Coast (miles)
howToSteps.description: Type or select your distance from coast in the field provided. Use the most accurate value available for best results.

howToSteps.title: Enter Your Timeframe (years)
howToSteps.description: Type or select your timeframe in the field provided. Use the most accurate value available for best results.

howToSteps.title: Click Calculate
howToSteps.description: Hit the Calculate button to run the numbers. Results appear instantly below.

howToSteps.title: Review Your Results
howToSteps.description: Check your projected rise, flood risk level, safety margin. Use these figures to inform your next decision or compare against alternative scenarios.

tips: Double-check your property elevation before calculating — even small input errors can significantly change your results.
Run the calculator with different values to compare scenarios and find the optimal approach for your situation.
Pay attention to both projected rise and flood risk level — they work together to give you the full picture.
Bookmark this page for quick access next time you need to get accurate results.
If you're unsure about your timeframe, start with a conservative estimate and adjust from there.
